---
title: "What Controls Mine Waste Volume in the U.S.?"
subtitle: "A Geological Analysis using USGS NMWI Data"
author: "Indigo Sterud"
institute: "University of Illinois at Chicago"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: xaringan-themer.css
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      ratio: "16:9"
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
---

```{r load-packages, include = FALSE}
# Add any additional packages you need to this chunk
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(knitr)
library(xaringanthemer)
library(readr)
library(kableExtra)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# For better figure resolution
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.retina = 3, dpi = 300, fig.width = 6, fig.asp = 0.618, out.width = "80%")
```

```{r load-data, include=FALSE}
Waste_Geology <- read_csv("/cloud/project/project-starter/data/Waste_Geology.csv")
Waste_Resources <- read_csv("/cloud/project/project-starter/data/Waste_Resources.csv")

mining_df <- left_join(Waste_Resources, Waste_Geology, by = "Site_ID") %>%
  rename(
    Site_Name = Site_Name.x,
    Site_Name_geo = Site_Name.y)

mining_clean <- mining_df %>%
  mutate(
    state_abbr = substr(Site_ID, 1, 2),
    state_name = state.name[match(state_abbr, state.abb)],
    log_vol = log10(Calc_Vol + 1)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(Calc_Vol, Ore_Min)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE}
style_xaringan(
  title_slide_background_image = "img/Innovation-in-mining.jpg"
)
```


## Research Questions


- Do certain mineral commodities produce more waste?

--

- Does geology (ore / gangue minerals) influence waste volume?

--

- Are there differences in waste production across states?

---

## Mining Data
- Columns represent different mine waste features for each Mine Location.
- Ore_Min = commodity minerals
- Gangue_Min = non-commodity minerals

```{r glimpse-table, echo = FALSE}
mining_clean %>%
  select(Site_Name, state_abbr, Calc_Vol, Ore_Min, Gangue_Min) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  kable(format = "html") %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    font_size = 10
  )
```

---

class: center, middle

# Differences in Mining Waste per State (Plot)

---

.pull-right[
```{r state-waste-log-plot, echo = FALSE}
ggplot(mining_clean, aes(x = reorder(state_abbr, Calc_Vol, FUN = median),y = Calc_Vol)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "darkgreen") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  labs( title = "Mine Waste Volume by State (Log Scale)", x = "State", y = "Log10 Waste Volume")
```
]

.pull-left[
```{r state-waste-regular, echo = FALSE}
ggplot(mining_clean, aes(x = reorder(state_abbr, Calc_Vol, FUN = median), y = Calc_Vol)) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue") + 
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) + 
  labs( title = "Mine Waste Volume Distribution by State", x = "State", y = "Waste Volume" )
```
]
---

class: inverse, middle, center

# Commodity Effects on Waste

- Do certain mineral commodities produce more waste?

---
```{r commodity-data, echo=FALSE}
# In this chunk I'm doing a bunch of analysis that I don't want to present 
# in my slides. But I need the resulting data frame for a plot I want to present.
Commodity_Data <- mining_clean %>%
  separate_rows(Commodity, sep = ",|;") %>%
  mutate(Commodity = trimws(Commodity)) %>%
  mutate(Commodity = gsub("\\s*\\(.*\\)", "", Commodity)) %>%
  group_by(Commodity) %>%
  summarize(median_vol = median(Calc_Vol, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  slice_max(median_vol, n = 20)
```

```{r commodity-plot, echo=FALSE}
Commodity_Plot <- Commodity_Data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Commodity, median_vol), y = median_vol)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkgreen") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "serif")) +
  labs(
    title = "Waste Volume by Commodity Mineral",
    x = "Ore Mineral",
    y = "Median Volume"
  )

Commodity_Plot
```

---

class: inverse, middle, center

# Gangue Minerals

- Does surrounding geology influence waste volume?

---

```{r gangue-plot, echo=FALSE}
Gangue_Plot <- mining_clean %>%
  separate_rows(Gangue_Min, sep = ",|;") %>%
  mutate(Gangue_Min = trimws(Gangue_Min)) %>%
  mutate(Gangue_Min = gsub("\\s*\\(.*\\)", "", Gangue_Min)) %>%
  group_by(Gangue_Min) %>%
  summarize(mean_vol = mean(Calc_Vol, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  slice_max(mean_vol, n = 15) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Gangue_Min, mean_vol), y = mean_vol)) +
  geom_col(fill = "brown") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "serif", size = 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "Waste Volume by Gangue Mineral",
    x = "Gangue Mineral",
    y = "Mean Volume"
  )

Gangue_Plot
```

---

# Layouts

You can use plain text

- or bullet points

.pull-left[
or text in two columns $^*$
]
.pull-right[
- like
- this
]

.footnote[
[*] And add footnotes
]

---

# Code

```{r boring-regression}
# a boring regression
model <- lm(dist ~ speed, data = cars)
tidy(model)
glance(model)
```

---

# Plots

```{r recode-species, echo = FALSE}
# In this chunk I'm doing a bunch of analysis that I don't want to present 
# in my slides. But I need the resulting data frame for a plot I want to present.
iris_modified <- iris %>%
  mutate(Species = fct_other(Species, keep = "setosa"))
```

```{r plot-iris, echo = FALSE}
# Code hidden with echo = FALSE
# Uses modified iris dataset from previous chunk
# Play around with height and width until you're happy with the look
ggplot(data = iris_modified, mapping = aes(x = Sepal.Width, y = Sepal.Length, color = Species)) +
  geom_point() + 
  theme_minimal() # theme options: https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/ggtheme.html
```

---

## Plot and text

.pull-left[
- Some text
- goes here
]
.pull-right[
```{r warning=FALSE, out.width="100%", fig.width=4, echo=FALSE}
# see how I changed out.width and fig.width from defaults
# to make the figure bigger
ggplot(penguins, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = species, color = species)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal()
```
]

---

# Tables

If you want to generate a table, make sure it is in the HTML format (instead of Markdown or other formats), e.g.,

```{r iris-table, echo = FALSE}
kable(head(iris), format = "html")
```

---

# Images

```{r castle, echo = FALSE, out.width = "60%", fig.align = "center", fig.cap = "Image credit: Photo by Enjoy Illinois."}
include_graphics("img/chicago-fall.jpeg")
```


---

# Feeling adventurous?

- Want to find out more about `xaringan`? See https://slides.yihui.name/xaringan/#1.

- You are welcomed to use the default styling of the slides. In fact, that's what I expect majority of you will do. You will differentiate yourself with the content of your presentation.

- But some of you might want to play around with slide styling. The 
`xaringanthemer` provides some solutions for this that: https://pkg.garrickadenbuie.com/xaringanthemer.

- And if you want more bells and whistles, there is also `xaringanExtra`: https://pkg.garrickadenbuie.com/xaringanExtra.
